findings.title: Deprecated Apache Kafka Migration Risk
findings.description: The current use of Apache Kafka 2.8, which is reaching end-of-life in Q1 2026, poses a critical risk due to known vulnerabilities and incompatibility with the KRaft protocol. This migration is essential to maintain system integrity and security.
assumptions: Migration to Kafka 3.7+ is feasible within the current architecture.

evidence.excerpt: Migrate from deprecated Apache Kafka 2.x to Kafka 3.7+

recommendations: Prioritize migration to Kafka 3.7+ in the MVP.

findings.title: Vendor Lock-in on Proprietary ETL SDK
findings.description: The reliance on VendorX's proprietary ETL SDK creates a dependency that is financially unsustainable due to the announced price increase. Evaluating open-source alternatives is critical to mitigate this risk.
assumptions: Open-source alternatives can meet current functionality requirements.

evidence.excerpt: Eliminate vendor lock-in on proprietary transformation engine

recommendations: Conduct a feasibility study on open-source alternatives for the V1.

findings.title: Python 3.9 EOL Migration Risk
findings.description: The need to upgrade from Python 3.9 to 3.12 poses a risk due to compatibility issues with existing dependencies, particularly numpy and custom C extensions. This could delay the migration process significantly.
assumptions: All services can be updated without major architectural changes.

evidence.excerpt: Python 3.9 EOL migration for all pipeline workers

recommendations: Plan for a phased upgrade of Python versions in V2.

findings.title: Redis Cluster Migration Dependency
findings.description: The current single-region Redis cluster is a bottleneck for performance and scalability. Migrating to a multi-region Redis 7+ setup is necessary to improve latency and avoid EOL issues.
assumptions: Multi-region Redis setup can be integrated with existing architecture.

evidence.excerpt: Replace single-region Redis cluster with multi-region caching

recommendations: Evaluate multi-region Redis solutions for V1.

findings.title: Dependency Health Dashboard Implementation
findings.description: Creating a centralized dependency health dashboard will allow proactive management of dependencies and vulnerabilities, reducing risks associated with EOL and CVE issues.
assumptions: The dashboard can be built using existing tools and integrations.

evidence.excerpt: Create dependency health dashboard and automated CVE alerting

recommendations: Implement the dashboard in the MVP to monitor dependencies.

summary: The analysis identifies critical risks associated with deprecated technologies and vendor lock-in, necessitating immediate migration efforts. A phased delivery plan is proposed, prioritizing essential migrations in the MVP, while addressing dependencies and risks in subsequent versions.
